frameworks.name: functional cell-type clustering
frameworks.one_line: lOFC neurons partition into discrete response clusters.

frameworks.name: generalized linear model
frameworks.one_line: Poisson GLM quantifies per-neuron encoding of task variables.

frameworks.name: corticostriatal projection identity
frameworks.one_line: Implicated cluster resembles identified corticostriatal cells.

frameworks.name: sequential-effects / trial-by-trial learning
frameworks.one_line: Reward-history integration as substrate for risk-history bias.

core_question: Do distinct functional subpopulations of lateral OFC neurons integrate previous and current rewards at the time of choice?
key_assumptions: Prefrontal response heterogeneity reduces to a few discrete clusters.
PSTH and conditional-feature clustering expose meaningful cell types.
CPD and mutual information validly index encoding strength.
manipulations: Trial-by-trial cued reward probability and reward volume.
Left/right side randomly assigned as risky (p<1) or safe (p=1).
key_findings: Both methods yield five lOFC clusters with substantial cross-method overlap on two.
Reward attributes weakly encoded; reward history, choice, and reward receipt strongly encoded across clusters.
Only one cluster encodes reward history shortly preceding choice (CPD, MI, d' agree).
That cluster also has the strongest current-outcome encoding and resembles corticostriatal cells.
interpretations: A specific lOFC subpopulation is positioned to integrate previous and current outcomes at choice, possibly mediating sequential learning.
lOFC behavioral flexibility may derive from dedicated cell types rather than uniform coding.
limitations_noted: Silhouette score inconclusive in this regime; gap statistic is conservative.
Projection target inferred by resemblance, not directly identified here.
